title: Peccati Syn
content:

{{Quote|Violence only ever begets more violence.|A mantra from Syn's religion, the Sacred Way|<ref name="sw7">[[Star Wars (1977) 7|''Star Wars'' (1977) 7]]</ref>}}
'''Peccati Syn''' was one of the twelve original [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Raised as a devout [[Sacred Way/Legends|Sacred Way]] follower, the young Syn was forced to abandon his religion under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] oppression. He joined the Empire to fill a spiritual void, rising through the ranks thanks to his tactical expertise and energetic devotion. After becoming a Grand Admiral in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], he fought with distinction in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Silooth (Star Destroyer)|Fi]]''.

When the Empire began to fragment after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Syn again searched for a new source of spiritual inspiration, and found one in the [[Church of the Dark Side]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. While officially remaining to Emperor [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage's]] faltering nominal reign, Syn's true loyalty was with the church and its Prophets, and most of his time was spent on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the residence of his spiritual guide, [[Merili]]. When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] launched a major offensive against Imperial-controlled Kashyyyk eight months after [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], Syn was embroiled in a tactical confrontation with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. Ultimately, the Grand Admiral lost the duel; he [[Death/Legends|died]] when his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was vaporized in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].

==Biography==
===Ascension===
The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] Peccati Syn was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]<ref name="warlords" /> into a deeply religious family, who devoutly followed<ref name="who" /> the pacifist, karmic<ref name="sw7" /> religion known as the [[Sacred Way/Legends|Sacred Way]],<ref name="who" /> and its [[Holy-Book]].<ref name="sw7" /> His parents instilled in him the belief that, should he adhere to his religion's tenets, he would reach a glorious, idyllic afterlife, and Syn lived a happy, devoted childhood. However, soon the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began to enforce its prohibition of religion on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="who" /> Some, such as the [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] "[[Pera]]" on [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]], ignored the mandate and continued to practice,<ref name="sw7" /> but Syn complied.<ref name="who" />

After such a faith-oriented youth, Syn was left spiritually barren, and, bereft of other options, turned to the doctrine of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] to fill this religious vacuum. He ardently internalized the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] dogma and enlisted in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], carving out a name for himself. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Syn was one of twelve men elevated by Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to the newly created and highly prestigious position of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]].<ref name="who" /> His appointment surprised many in the Empire as there were numerous [[Admiral/Legends|admirals]] with comparable service records.<ref name="TEGW" /> Syn's ascension coincided with the beginning of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], and he fought the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] with devotion to duty and tactical acumen. He named his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] ''[[Silooth (Star Destroyer)|Fi]]'', after the [[High Galactic (language)|High Galactic]] [[Fi (High Galactic)|word]] for "son" used by Sacred Way clergymen.<ref name="who" /> Syn became close friends with fellow Tarisian Admiral [[Foga Brill/Legends|Foga Brill]], whom he helped attain [[Governor/Legends|governorship]] of the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] planet [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]].<ref name="warlords" />

===Imperial fragmentation===
Syn's exalted Empire began to deteriorate after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], in which Emperor Palpatine and his enforcer [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] [[Death/Legends|perished]], along with the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] and the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. Many Imperials, including some of Syn's Grand Admiral colleagues, turned [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], seizing personal dominions,<ref name="who" /> but Syn continued to defend his areas of operation and complied with the orders coming out of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="TEGW" /> Former [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] was technically Interim-Emperor, though he vied for control with the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] and [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], among others. One of Pestage's acts was to sanction the establishment of the [[Church of the Dark Side]] on Coruscant; overseen by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], it preached the renewal and regeneration of the Empire.<ref name="who" />

Like the hordes of spiritually-starved citizens on the Imperial capital, Syn took to the church fervently,<ref name="who" /> even converting his friend Foga Brill to the religion;<ref name="warlords" /> no sooner than his Imperial religion had become untenable, a ready-made replacement had cropped up. In truth, the Church was a sham headed by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] impersonators, founded by Emperor Pestage and [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Cronal]] in a desperate attempt to strengthen the former Vizier's weakening clutch on the Empire. Nevertheless, Syn was rejuvenated, and while he publicly supported Pestage's regime, his true loyalty lay with the Prophets. The Grand Admiral even attempted in vain to hearten his dispirited colleague [[Ishin-Il-Raz]], lending him a copy of the sacred book ''[[Secrets of the Dark Side]]''.<ref name="who" />

===Demise===
Syn operated out of Imperial-controlled [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the base of Prophetess [[Merili]]. Merili was one of the few genuine Prophets working within the Church of the Dark Side, and she became Syn's spiritual advisor. Entrenched in the church's doctrine, Syn vowed not to turn his back on another religion, and served the church's ends along with ''Fi'' and its crew.<ref name="who" /> Eight months after the Battle of Endor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] embarked on an ambitious offensive into Imperial territory, led by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. Kashyyyk was targeted; Ackbar brought his [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to bear against Syn's in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over the planet.<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

Assured by two [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] who claimed to be working for the Imperial warlord [[Treuten Teradoc]] that reinforcements were being sent from the [[Greater Maldrood]], Syn [[Liberation of Kashyyyk/Legends|engaged]] Ackbar's fleet, unaware that the Bothans were actually New Republic agents and had sabotaged two of his [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]].<ref name="TEGW" /> Locked in a one-on-one tactical confrontation with Ackbar,<ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> the Grand Admiral, fighting with his newfound spiritual resoluteness, did his best, with no concern for his personal well-being or that of his crew.<ref name="who" /> Ultimately, the New Republic outfought their Imperial counterparts,<ref name="nec" /> and Ackbar won the tactical duel.<ref name="negtc" /> The [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']] and [[Maria (MC80 Star Cruiser)|''Maria'']], commanded by Ackbar and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Verrack/Legends|Verrack]] respectively, caught Syn's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]—now named the ''Silooth''—in a crossfire and destroyed its [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] with a volley of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]],<ref name="TEGW" /> taking with it Syn, his crew,<ref name="who" /> and Merili.<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The Church did not outlast the late Grand Admiral for long,<ref name="nec" /> and soon after wresting control of the Imperial remnants from Pestage, Ysanne Isard reinstated freedom of religion, legalizing the Sacred Way.<ref name="who" />

==Personality and traits==
Peccati Syn had fair skin, [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and graying [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="who" /> His life was marked by his single-minded devotion to whatever cause he joined. As a youth, he never strayed from the Sacred Way, and experienced his happiest and fondest times in life. When the time came to replace that religion with the New Order, however, he did so with similar devotion, serving with distinction,<ref name="who" /> despite the fact that the Empire's ethos clashed strongly with that of the Sacred Way, which preached pacifism and divine retribution for sins.<ref name="sw7" /> Syn, however, did not forget his roots, naming his flagship after a word used by the Sacred Way's clergymen when addressing laypeople. After the collapse of the Empire as known and cherished by Syn, he again found solace in another religion, this time the Church of the Dark Side. The church was a fake, though, and Syn himself was duped.<ref name="who" />

Unlike many of his peers, Syn did not join the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] out of self-importance or barbarity, but rather out of a perceived necessity, in the absence of virtually any other religious organizations.<ref name="who" /> Although his lofty rank initially took many by surprise,<ref name="TEGW" /> Syn demonstrated his tireless, dutiful work and skill in the war against the Rebel Alliance. After Endor, even having lost faith in the Empire, Syn made no attempts to claim a personal fiefdom, and for the rest of his life remained ostensibly loyal to Coruscant.<ref name="who" />

Peccati Syn was far less morally bankrupt than many of his colleagues, who committed atrocities with the aim of gaining power or prestige, or to fulfill personal ambitions. Until his final engagement at Kashyyyk, he went into battle with concern for the well being of his subordinates, while he even attempted to coax Ishin-Il-Raz, an extremely unpopular fellow Grand Admiral, out of his depression; meanwhile, many of his Imperial peers were clashing over disputed territories and scheming in shadows.<ref name="who" /> Syn was also close friends with Admiral Foga Brill, with whom he enjoyed holding theological debates—Syn helped Brill gain a governorship, and eventually coaxed him into joining the Church of the Dark Side.<ref name="warlords" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|His first name "Peccatti" ''[sic]'' is Italian for "sins," so I suppose this Imperial is doubly sinful. No wonder Admiral Ackbar blows his arse away.|Abel G. Peña|<ref name="blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|3|The Tongue Gods Love Garbage... Or, The Dark Forces Saga Continues!}}</ref>}}
Grand Admiral Syn was created by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] for [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] ([[2000]]), where his defeat to Ackbar is briefly mentioned. His name and title appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' as a leader for the Imperial faction in standard matches. Syn was briefly mentioned again in Wallace's ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' ([[2002]]), before Wallace expanded upon the character's history with co-author [[Abel G. Peña]] in ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', published in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]] ([[2003]]), wherein his first name is established. ''Who's Who'' was originally due to appear in ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine, but after ''Gamer''{{'s}} cancellation, it was moved to ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', sans gaming statistics. The move also necessitated the article to be condensed;<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref> as a result, some of the Grand Admirals' biographical information was cut, though Syn's remained mostly intact. The cut content revealed that Syn renamed his flagship ''Silooth'' after a [[Silooth/Legends|type]] of [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]]; although originally cut,<ref name="dd">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref> ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ([[2012]]) confirmed that the ship was called this at the time of Syn's death. The Grand Admiral has also been referenced in Wallace's ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ([[2005]]) and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]).

The Italian word "''peccati''" means "sins" in English, while "syn" bears similarity to "sin". This is an intentional pun on the part of ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals'' coauthor [[Abel G. Peña]], who is conversant in Italian.<ref name="blog" />

{{Cutstart}}
===Cut content===
Syn's future pointed to him becoming a "Pera" upon reaching adulthood, but after the Empire intervened and Syn was told to discard his beliefs, this dream was forgotten. Instead, he became a Grand Admiral, but in the course of his campaign rooting out Rebels during the Galactic Civil War, his conscience was tested. His guilt at abandoning his beliefs prompted him to turn a blind eye to atrocities being committed within the Empire in the name of religion, like in the case of the [[Yevetha]] or [[Dim-U/Legends|Dim-U]]. When the Empire began to crumble, Syn was reminded of morbid stories about [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] from his childhood, and was quick to turn to the newly founded Church of the Dark Side. As a devotee of the Prophets, Syn regained his childhood happiness; he symbolically renamed his flagship ''Silooth'', after a type of Sithspawn, to represent his new faith. During the Second Battle of Kashyyyk, Syn saw himself defending the church, not the Empire—he died a happy man, fighting for a cause he could believe in.<ref name="dd" />
{{Cutend}}
